title: 1/31/20 Republicans tore tongues so activate comments to counter!

transcript.text: Hello, good morning. January. I live in Boulder, Vlog. January 31st. 2020 Friday at 10.04 in the morning. So yeah, this is when I'm getting out of bed. I just brushed my teeth. That's the only thing that I did so far.

Um, I set the alarm for six, but it was freezing last night and I was freezing and I was like, I don't think so. Um, and then I just laid in bed until I felt like it was warm enough. And then I got up at 10 and walking around. I don't know. I feel kind of tired.

Uh, I didn't do like yesterday. It wasn't a full day of training because I, uh, most of it or half of it was just trying to get caught up on my life again. Some more. Um, so yeah, I got up at like nine 30 yesterday and I think the first thing I did was do the erg swimmer thing for two K and like, I do it in 38 minutes. Um, but like,

Like my lats are not activated yet. There's no power at all on my lats. So I don't bother with my lats. Lats, like the bottom part. I only do the shoulder part. I'm only doing this part. I want to get this, this part built up. My left shoulder was kind of sore. I don't know. Um, so yeah. And then I went and stretched for a half hour.
Um, yeah, I did that three times a day. Three times yesterday. I did two kilometers swimming, stretched a half hour, two kilometers stretch, two kilometers stretch.

And then at the end of the stretching, it was eight 20. So it's time for me to lay in bed. I'm trying to become a diurnalist. Um, I feel a little bit tired today. Um, but yeah, so I, maybe I shouldn't have done any more than that.

I looked at my Strava and my very first day of exercise was on the 18th of January. So, and it's, we're still in January. So like I literally just started, it's been less than two weeks since I got off my ass. So, you know, I should expect to be struggling for at least the first month.
Um, just considering the situation that I've been in, how long it's been since I've done everything.
Just riding my bike and feeling my belly. And I was like, dang, you're fat, but you know, manageable, but I'm thinking, Ooh, that's the fattest you'll ever be. Hopefully. So, um, um, yeah.

The other things that I did that filled up my day where I had to organize some more and then I had to, um, I mean, I'm kind of drawing a blank. The thing that took the most time out of my day was cleaning my gecko cage, which I hadn't done in forever.

My geckos like desperate need of having their cage cleaned. Um, and the day, a couple of days ago or the day before I spent a lot of time cleaning my fish cage too. So when my geckos die, I'm not going to get more geckos.
I bought those geckos when I was living in my living, just working on my website all the time. And, uh, I've had like five years, you're supposed to live five years.
I have a male and a female and I want to make sure to take care of real good of them. Now. I think I accidentally have put too many crickets in there.
The females eyes are fused shut. I hope that they didn't get pecked out or infected from the, by crickets or infected by the dirty cage.
Cause that made me feel as bad as my fault. Her nose is all bloody from getting attacked by the male.

And I, but I've got a bunch of worms and, uh, I go, I got the new vitamins in there. I haven't had those in there for a couple of months. So yeah, maybe I have too many crickets, but the male's pretty good at scaring away the crickets from the inside of their hide.
Anyway. Um, and then I, yeah, so I went down to the store and I, I bought, um, the, the, the vitamins for them and crickets and worms everywhere I go. People go, Oh my God, nice hair.
Every time I take a shower though, all this red comes out. So I don't know how much longer this bright red is going to last. Plus my hair is growing out into it.
Um, then I went to Whole Foods and got, um, stuff to clean my window and, uh, paper towels. Cause every, or my mirror, every time I, I swim, I look down at myself and watch myself do it.
But then all the sweat gets on the mirror. So I have to clean the mirror. The mirror gets filthy every time.
Um, I still haven't gotten around to looking at my bookshelf over there and making sure everything is organized.
So I'm still in organized mode. I'll do that. But, uh, another thing that I had to do, I rode my bike down to the pet store yesterday on my e-bike.
I have a really nice road bike with, uh, you know, brand new brake lever, you know, those brake lever shifters and, um, uh, yeah, brand new everything, uh, with a Copenhagen wheel on it.

Uh, I'm going to use it for running up in the mountains this summer so I can ride my bike way, way, way, way, way up to the Continental Divide and like climb a mountain and then ride the bike all the way back.
Um, make it a whole lot easier and I could do a lot bigger runs up there if I have an e-bike up there.
I think the e-bike battery will last all the way to the mountains.
I'm pretty sure, especially after I lose weight and I get better rider.
Um, so yeah, it'll be interesting to see if it, if it does, but I think it will.
And then it kind of, it recharges on the way down.
So, um, that'll be interesting.
I'm really looking forward to vlogging about that.
That's like a new lifestyle.
I think riding e-bikes up to the mountains and running in the mountains and come back, especially for the triathletes because triathletes want to, uh, want to train at high altitude.
And so if you're running at like 12,000 feet every day, but you live in Boulder and you don't even have to drive up there, you ride your bike up there.
That seems awesome.
So, um, but, uh, there was a problem, you know, I bought that bike for my girlfriend and we were here, she was here last summer and it would work for like five minutes and then turn off.
And so I just wanted to see what was going on because I bought a demagnetizer.
I heard that's what's wrong with it.
So I rode down in the store and it worked great on the way down there.
But as soon as I stopped pedaling it, you know, it wasn't working anymore.
So I had to ride home without it helping me, which is fine.
You can ride it just fine without helping you.
It just makes it heavier.
There's no resistance on the wheel.
But, um, so then I demagnetize it.
It's this thing you plug in and you push the button and you hold it really close to it and you feel a vibration and then you pull it away.
And so, and then I turn it on, turn it on.
It's like, you need to recalibrate.
So, and then it was working.
So I don't know, maybe I fixed it.
Um, it got demagnetized when I took the wheel off.
I had it as a regular bike.
I have a regular wheel, real wheel on it.
So I was using it as a regular bicycle or I just put a regular wheel on to see how it worked.
I bought a regular wheel for it.
And when you switch out the wheels, it demagnetizes it.
So I guess if I get a flat and I have to switch my wheel with my electric bike, with my Copenhagen wheel, then I can't expect for it to be, um, to work after I fix the flat.
Cause it'll be, get demagnetized or at least not work after the first five minutes.
So we'll see.
Um, um, so, um, that's another thing that took time.
And then I had to switch the, um, the stem cause I had the stem fit for my five foot four, five foot five girlfriend.
So then I put the bigger stem on there to make the bike fit me.
So now we're good to go.
Um, and, um, yeah, so that was like the day.
Um, I think I did some other organizational stuff, but that was pretty much my day.
So, um, today, uh, I, I feel a little bit lethargic.
So I don't know if I'm a little bit overcooked or what, but what I'm going to do is, uh, you know, for the first month, I'm going to expect to be struggling.
And we're not even at the 18th of, we're not even close to the 18th of February yet, which will be my one month anniversary of exercising.
So, uh, yeah, don't, don't, you know, don't get too hippity hippity hype.
Um, I'm going to, I think, uh, do swimming today on the erg machine, not in a bowl.
I was going to, my mom called me at like eight, seven this morning to go to the gym, but I couldn't get out of bed.
I, I, I, I couldn't get out of bed because I was freezing.
I, I brought a, uh, too many blankets.
I did a purge in my house and brought stuff to my mom's house in the basement.
And I, I brought a, too many blankets over there.
So I was cold.
I was freezing last night.
So that's the one reason I had to sleep.
So I have to go back to her house to grab my blankets again.
Uh, yeah, that's cold.
It really speaks up on you.
But my heater doesn't work.
Soon we stopped working.
So I need to call home when it's on.
So, um, oops, past nine minutes.
Um, yeah.
So, uh, um, I'm going to, I, I only ate a sandwich yesterday.
I had a protein powder like three times, but the only thing that I actually ate was a sandwich.
So I'm, my appetite still hasn't even kicked in yet.
So I'll probably, I think I'll have a protein drink and maybe have a little bit of cereal.
I think I have some cereal.
And then I think today from now on, I'm going to start my day by a half hour stretching instead of a half hour on the exercising.
And then I'll do the ERG 2K and then I'll stretch a half hour and then the ERG 2K and maybe I'll ride.
It just depends.
Uh, yeah, until eight o'clock.
And then I'll, um, I'll, uh, maybe go to my mom's house for the blanket or else I'll, uh, sleep in a bunch of clothes and try to, try to see if I can get up at six tomorrow.
That's, I hate trying to get to sleep because I had insomnia all night last night.
Like all night until like five.
All right, later.
Talk to you tomorrow.
Talk to you tomorrow.
